title: Expressing support for the designation of February 4, 2025, as "Transit Equity Day".
plain_summary: This congressional resolution wants to make February 4, 2025 a special day called Transit Equity Day. The day would honor Rosa Parks, who fought for equal rights on buses and other public transportation. The resolution recognizes that some people still do not have fair access to public transportation today. It encourages people to use public transit and supports efforts to make buses and trains available to everyone.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Highways and Transit.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution shows support for making February fourth, two thousand twenty five, a special day called Transit Equity Day.

sections.title: Background Facts
sections.text: Rosa Parks was an important person who fought for social justice and racial equality. She helped start the civil rights movement that ended legal separation of races in the United States. Rosa Parks famous protest was about unequal access to public buses and trains for African Americans. Today, unequal access to public transportation based on race, income, and disability still exists. This problem has gotten worse because of cuts to transportation funding and higher fares on many transit systems. Paratransit is a special type of public transportation for people with disabilities and should be included when public transit is expanded. Affordable and reliable public transportation is an essential public service, just as important as water and electricity. Using more public transportation can help fight climate change by reducing car pollution that hurts communities near highways and major roads, which are often communities of color and economically disadvantaged communities. Many communities across the country celebrate Transit Equity Day on February fourth, which is Rosa Parks birthday, by offering free rides on public transportation.

sections.title: What the House of Representatives Resolves to Do
sections.text: The House of Representatives honors the life and work of Rosa Parks by officially recognizing Transit Equity Day across the nation. The House encourages people to use public transportation and supports continued work to make public transit available to everyone. The House praises transit agencies across the country that offer free rides on Transit Equity Day. The House asks that the Clerk of the House of Representatives send an official copy of this resolution to the co-executive directors of the Labor Network for Sustainability, Liz Ratzloff and Joshua Dedmond, and to the convener of the National Campaign for Transit Justice, LeeAnn Hall.
